# immoscout

> Typed, unofficial Python client for ImmobilienScout24 (immobilienscout24.de). Search real-estate listings, fetch expose details, and expose it all to AI agents via a built-in Model Context Protocol (MCP) server. `pip install immoscout`.

`immoscout` wraps ImmobilienScout24's mobile API and returns clean, typed Pydantic
objects instead of raw JSON. Because it uses the mobile API endpoints, it needs no
headless browser, no HTML scraping, and no captcha solving — the website shows captchas
to scripted browsers, the mobile endpoints just return JSON. It is designed to be both a normal Python library and
an agent-native tool: it ships an MCP server so assistants like Claude can search
real estate natively. Unofficial and not affiliated with ImmobilienScout24; for
personal/educational use, respecting their Terms of Service.

## Install

- `pip install immoscout` — library + CLI
- `pip install "immoscout[mcp]"` — also installs the MCP server

## Core usage

```python
from immoscout import ImmoscoutClient, SearchFilter

client = ImmoscoutClient()

# Search with typed filters -> SearchResult of typed Listing objects
result = client.search(region="/de/berlin/berlin", price_max=1200, rooms_min=2)
for listing in result.listings:
    print(listing.title, listing.price, listing.rooms, listing.living_space, listing.url)

# Auto-paginate across all pages (one request per page)
for listing in client.search_all(SearchFilter(region="/de/bayern/muenchen", price_max=1500), max_pages=3):
    ...

# Full details for one listing
expose = client.get_expose(result.listings[0].id)
print(expose.price, expose.address, expose.description)
```

Every model keeps the untouched API payload on `.raw`.

## Key objects

- `ImmoscoutClient(user_agent=..., timeout=15, max_retries=3, backoff=0.5)` — sync client with timeouts, retries/backoff, and rate-limit handling.
  - `.search(filter_or_kwargs) -> SearchResult`
  - `.count(filter_or_kwargs) -> int` — total matches only, no listings fetched.
  - `.search_all(filter, max_pages=None) -> Iterator[Listing]`
  - `.get_expose(expose_id) -> Expose`
  - `.suggest_regions(query, limit=10) -> list[GeoLocation]` — resolve a place name (e.g. "münchen") to region paths ("/de/bayern/muenchen").
- `region` accepts a geocode path OR a plain place name; plain names are auto-resolved via suggest_regions before searching/counting.
- Full documentation site: https://wiestju.github.io/ImmoScout/
- `SearchFilter(region, real_estate_type, price_min, price_max, rooms_min, rooms_max, living_space_min, living_space_max, page, extra)` — validated query; `.to_params()` compiles to API params.
- `RealEstateType` — enum: apartmentrent, apartmentbuy, houserent, housebuy, flatshareroom, garagerent, garagebuy.
- `Listing` — id, title, url, address(line/lat/lon), price, currency, living_space, rooms, real_estate_type, published, is_private, is_new, picture_url, raw.
- `Expose` — id, title, address, price, rooms, living_space, attributes(dict), description, url, raw.
- Exceptions: `ImmoscoutError` (base), `RequestError`, `NotFoundError`, `RateLimitError`.

## CLI

- `immoscout regions <query>` — look up region geocode paths by name
- `immoscout search --region Berlin --price-max 1200 --rooms-min 2 [--json]`
- `immoscout count --region Berlin --price-max 1000` — number of matches only
- `immoscout expose <expose_id>`

## MCP server (AI agents)

- Run `immoscout-mcp` (needs the `[mcp]` extra), or configure it as an MCP server (command `immoscout-mcp`) in Claude Desktop / Claude Code / Cursor.
- Tools: `search_listings(...)`, `count_listings(...)`, `get_expose(expose_id)`, and `suggest_regions(query)`.

## Notes

- Regions are ImmoScout geocode paths, e.g. `/de/berlin/berlin`, `/de/bayern/muenchen`.
- Range filters use `min-max` semantics; open-ended ranges like `50.0-` (min only) are supported.
- Be gentle with request volume; ImmobilienScout24 rate-limits and blocks aggressive traffic.
